title: Gibbesia rugelii
taxonomy: Gibbesia rugelii is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant is known by several common names, including mountain apple and wild apple. Gibbesia rugelii is often mistaken for its close relative, Annona glabra, but they can be distinguished by their different leaf shapes and sizes. Gibbesia rugelii is typically found in woodland areas and along streams, where it can grow as a small shrub or tree.

intro: Gibbesia rugelii, also known as Rugel's blue sage or desert sage, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. Bioactive constituents of Gibbesia rugelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Gibbesia rugelii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
